creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's looming IPO poised to reshape the landscape for everyone.
contentSnippet: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's looming IPO poised to reshape the landscape for everyone.

creator: Amanda Silberling
title: (No AI) OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
content: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.
contentSnippet: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

title: (No AI) Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
author: Jay Peters
content:
![The OpenClaw logo on a dark blue background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/02/STKB382_OPEN_CLAW_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw," according to an email sent to users on Friday evening. Instead, if users want to use OpenClaw with Claude, they'll have to use a "pay-as-you-go option" that will be billed separate from their Claude subscription.

With OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger now [employed by OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/879623/openclaw-founder-peter-steinberger-joins-openai), Anthropic may also be encouraging subscribers to use [more of its own tools, like Claude Cowork, instead](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899430/anthropic-claude-code-cowork-ai-control-computer). Steinber …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/907074/anthropic-openclaw-claude-subscription-ban)

Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw," according to an email sent to users on Friday evening. Instead, if users want to use OpenClaw with Claude, they'll have to use a "pay-as-you-go option" that will be billed separate from their Claude subscription. 
With OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger now employed by OpenAI, Anthropic may also be encouraging subscribers to use more of its own tools, like Claude Cowork, instead. Steinber …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/gettyimages-1239231852.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI is undergoing another round of C-suite changes, according to an internal memo viewed by _The Verge_.

Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment - who was until recently the company's CEO of applications - says in the memo that she will be stepping away on medical leave "for the next several weeks" due to a neuroimmune condition. While she's out, OpenAI president Greg Brockman will be in charge of product, including leading OpenAI's super app efforts. On the business side, CSO Jason Kwon, CFO Sarah Friar, and CRO Denise Dresser will take charge.

OpenAI's CMO, Kate Rouch, has also decided to step down in order to focus on her health, …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/906965/openais-agi-boss-is-taking-a-leave-of-absence)

title: (No AI) OpenAI acquires TBPN
content: OpenAI acquires TBPN to accelerate global conversations around AI and support independent media, expanding dialogue with builders, businesses, and the broader tech community.
contentSnippet: OpenAI acquires TBPN to accelerate global conversations around AI and support independent media, expanding dialogue with builders, businesses, and the broader tech community.

title: (No AI) Codex now offers more flexible pricing for teams
content: Codex now includes pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise, providing teams a more flexible option to start and scale adoption.
contentSnippet: Codex now includes pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise, providing teams a more flexible option to start and scale adoption.

title: (No AI) Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable

creator: Andy Greenberg, Dell Cameron, Maddy Varner, Andrew Couts
title: (No AI) Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
dc:creator: Andy Greenberg, Dell Cameron, Maddy Varner, Andrew Couts
content: Plus: The FBI says a recent hack of its wiretap tools poses a national security risk, attackers stole Cisco source code as part of an ongoing supply chain hacking spree, and more.

creator: Maxwell Zeff, Zoë Schiffer, Lily Hay Newman
title: (No AI) Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff, Zoë Schiffer, Lily Hay Newman
content: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.
contentSnippet: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.
